title: Write a Pod with Probes and Resource Limits
description: Write a Pod manifest with resource requests/limits, a liveness probe, and a readiness probe.
starterCode:
# Write a Pod manifest that:
# 1. Has name: nginx-pod in namespace: default
# 2. Uses image: nginx:1.27 with containerPort: 80
# 3. Sets resources:
#    requests: cpu 100m, memory 128Mi
#    limits: cpu 200m, memory 256Mi
# 4. Adds livenessProbe (httpGet path: /, port: 80, initialDelaySeconds: 15, periodSeconds: 20)
# 5. Adds readinessProbe (httpGet path: /, port: 80, initialDelaySeconds: 5, periodSeconds: 10)

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx-pod
  namespace: default
spec:
  containers:
    - name: nginx
      image: nginx:1.27
      # Add ports, resources, livenessProbe, readinessProbe
solutionCode:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx-pod
  namespace: default
spec:
  containers:
    - name: nginx
      image: nginx:1.27
      ports:
        - containerPort: 80
      resources:
        requests:
          cpu: "100m"
          memory: "128Mi"
        limits:
          cpu: "200m"
          memory: "256Mi"
      livenessProbe:
        httpGet:
          path: /
          port: 80
        initialDelaySeconds: 15
        periodSeconds: 20
      readinessProbe:
        httpGet:
          path: /
          port: 80
        initialDelaySeconds: 5
        periodSeconds: 10
hints: CPU resources use millicores: 100m = 0.1 CPU core
Probes use httpGet, tcpSocket, or exec as check methods
initialDelaySeconds lets the container start before first probe

simulatedOutput.success:
pod/nginx-pod created

NAME        READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
nginx-pod   1/1     Running   0          30s

Readiness probe: PASS (http GET / :80)
Liveness probe: PASS (http GET / :80)
Resources: cpu=100m/200m, memory=128Mi/256Mi
simulatedOutput.failure: Pod validation failed. Check resource requests/limits, livenessProbe, and readinessProbe syntax.
